html:
AI inside, Inc. (TSE:4488): ¥2,323, ¥9.3B market cap, May 15 close

AI inside is not the broken-MBO orphan described in the prior drafts. I found no evidence of a recent failed ¥3,400 to ¥3,800 tender offer, and M&A Online shows no TOB record for 4488. The real setup is cleaner and more interesting: a founder-controlled, ignored Japanese micro-cap AI software company with a real installed base, high gross margins, net cash, low churn, and a newly launched enterprise AI-agent platform that the market is still valuing mostly as slow-growth OCR.

The core business is DX Suite, Japan’s No. 1 AI-OCR/document automation product by Deloitte Tohmatsu MIC Research’s 2025 OCR market report. This is not glamorous frontier AI, but it sits exactly where Japanese enterprises still bleed labor: paper, PDFs, invoices, insurance forms, bank documents, medical referrals, government files, legacy workflows, and back-office data entry. In FY03/26, AI inside reported ¥4.75B revenue, 82% gross margin, ¥311M operating profit, ¥487M ordinary profit, ¥351M net income, and ¥818M operating cash flow. Recurring revenue was ¥4.49B, 94.5% of sales, up 7.1%. Contracts rose to 3,203, users rose 17.3% to 77,975, monthly read items rose 23.8%, and churn stayed low at 0.66%.

The reason the stock is cheap is obvious: reported growth is not spectacular. FY03/27 guidance is only ¥5.23B revenue, up 10.2%, with net income up just 3.0% to ¥362M. On headline PER, the stock is around 25x forward earnings, which does not screen as deep value. But that misses the two important points. First, AI inside has ¥4.77B of cash and deposits against ¥1.0B of borrowings, so net cash is roughly ¥3.77B, or about ¥941 per share. The operating business is being valued at about ¥5.5B, or only 1.1x FY03/27 sales and 1.1x guided recurring revenue. Second, the guided operating profit jumps 68% to ¥523M despite continued investment in R&D, sales, and the Leapnet launch. Ex-cash, the stock is roughly 10.6x forward operating profit and about 7x FY03/26 operating cash flow. That is cheap for a high-gross-margin recurring software asset if the new product cycle is real.

The variant perception is that AI inside is not just an AI-OCR stock. Management is trying to convert DX Suite from document extraction into an AI-agent execution layer. In October 2025, it implemented its in-house LLM PolySphere-4 into DX Suite, claiming 99.6% average OCR accuracy on real-world data including non-standard and handwritten documents. In the FY03/26 presentation, management said PolySphere-4 drove non-standard form read items up 405% year over year and pushed monthly read items above 400M. That matters because the bear case for OCR is commoditization by Microsoft, Google, and generic multimodal models. AI inside’s answer is not “we read characters better.” It is “we own the Japanese document workflow, the customer-specific training data, the downstream integration, and now the agent layer that acts on the document.”

The bigger call option is Leapnet, formally launched May 12, 2026. Leapnet turns internal enterprise data into RAG databases and lets users build no-code AI agents deployable through API. Pricing is usage-based at ¥2 per 1,000 tokens. This is a much larger TAM than OCR and potentially a natural upsell into the existing DX Suite base. AI inside is also pushing “Sovereign Grid,” an effort to deploy AI inside Cube hardware plus Leapnet into domestic data centers as a Japanese inference network. This may prove too ambitious for a ¥9B company, but it gives the stock unusual torque: the market is valuing a profitable OCR SaaS company, while management is trying to create a domestic enterprise AI stack across application, platform, and infrastructure.

Japan’s policy backdrop helps. The Digital Agency is rolling out Government AI “GENAI” as a FY2026 pilot for roughly 180,000 government employees across ministries, with full-scale implementation targeted from FY2027. That does not make AI inside a government winner by default, but it validates the direction of travel: Japanese enterprises and public bodies are moving from AI trials into operational deployment, and domestic, secure, Japanese-language AI infrastructure has strategic appeal.

The ownership and trading setup are also part of the mispricing. Founder/CEO Taku Toguchi owns about 47%, while retail brokerage accounts and small holders dominate much of the remainder. Liquidity is thin: May 15 volume was only 16,900 shares, or ¥39.7M. Yahoo Finance sentiment is split, and margin data shows 286,600 shares of margin longs against only 300 shares short, a 955x credit ratio. This is not an institutional consensus long. It is a controlled, illiquid, underfollowed Growth Market name with frustrated retail holders. That creates downside air pockets, but it also means a few clean quarters of Leapnet traction or a credible shareholder return plan could move the stock violently.

The cleanest way to underwrite the stock is not to assume heroic AI-agent adoption. The base case is that DX Suite keeps growing high single digits, Leapnet contributes modestly in FY03/27, gross margin stays near 80%, and operating margin recovers toward 10% to 12% as the old goodwill drag is gone and productivity improves. On FY03/27 guidance, recurring revenue is expected to rise to ¥4.89B, contracts to 3,895, and total revenue to ¥5.23B. Put a modest 2.2x EV/sales multiple on that revenue, add net cash, and the equity is worth roughly ¥3,800 to ¥4,000 per share. That is not an aggressive SaaS multiple. It is a small-company, Japan-control discount multiple applied to a sticky, profitable AI workflow asset.

The bull case is that the market realizes OCR is the wedge, not the endpoint. If Leapnet begins converting existing DX Suite customers into token-based AI-agent usage, revenue growth can reaccelerate from 10% toward 20% plus without a proportional rise in headcount. The company has 3,000 plus customer relationships, 77,000 plus users, deep Japanese document data, and a clear pain point. If Leapnet or Sovereign Grid shows even early partner traction, the valuation can move from 1.1x forward sales to 3x to 4x sales quickly, especially given the float. A strategic buyer also makes sense over time: a Japanese SIer, printing/document workflow company, data-center operator, or enterprise software vendor could value AI inside’s installed base, domestic model assets, and government/regulated-enterprise positioning more highly than public markets do.

The bear case is also straightforward. AI-OCR may be a melting category. Generic multimodal models are getting better fast, and large cloud vendors can bundle document extraction into broader enterprise platforms. Leapnet could be late, underdistributed, or too small to matter. Sovereign Grid could require capital and management attention without producing returns. Founder control and zero dividend mean minority holders may wait a long time for capital allocation discipline. FY03/26 operating profit also benefited from ¥185M of subsidy income at the ordinary-profit line, so reported ordinary earnings should not be treated as fully normalized. If sales stay stuck around 5% to 8% growth and AI-agent products fail to scale, the market will value this as a niche OCR vendor, not an AI platform.

But at ¥2,323, that disappointment is partly priced in. The stock is not “cheap” because the PER is low. It is cheap because the enterprise value is only about one year of recurring revenue for an 82% gross-margin, net-cash, cash-generating Japanese AI workflow company that has just launched its next product cycle. The upside case only requires investors to believe that AI inside can defend its workflow niche and attach AI-agent usage to an existing customer base. The market is still underwriting the old OCR business.

Bear target: ¥1,500, down 35%. DX Suite slows, Leapnet fails to matter, operating profit disappoints, and the stock trades near cash plus a heavily discounted software business.

Base target: ¥4,000, up 72%. FY03/27 guidance is met, recurring revenue approaches ¥4.9B, operating profit recovery is visible, and the market rerates the business to roughly 2.2x EV/sales.

Bull target: ¥7,000, up 201%. Leapnet proves real, AI-agent revenue accelerates, Sovereign Grid or a major partner validates the platform, and 4488 rerates to 3.5x to 4.0x forward sales plus net cash.
